candidates.item: Founded in 1911 by Hull House reformers, SGA helps children, families and communities facing great challenges to realize their potential.
candidates.why_interesting: SGA is one of Chicago's oldest human-service agencies — 115 years old — and traces its direct lineage to the Hull House reform movement that gave rise to Social Security and the U.S. juvenile court system. That origin story is genuinely striking: the same civic energy that reshaped American social policy is still running free youth programs on Chicago's South and West sides today. Not a generic nonprofit founding date — a specific, famous intellectual lineage.
candidates.recency_note: Stable historical fact; confirmed in multiple current source listings.

candidates.item: After merging with the Scholarship Association for Jewish Children, the organization became the Scholarship and Guidance Association (SGA), expanding its mission to address emotional and social challenges.
candidates.why_interesting: SGA's name is actually an acronym for 'Scholarship and Guidance Association,' which itself came from a merger with a Jewish children's scholarship group. Today it serves Chicago's predominantly Black and Latino South/West side neighborhoods — a quietly interesting cross-cultural institutional lineage that is almost invisible in their current branding.
candidates.recency_note: Stable historical fact on their own About page.

candidates.item: Pedott, the advertising legend behind the Chia Pet and the Clapper, was assisted in his youth by SGA.
candidates.why_interesting: The inventor of the Chia Pet and The Clapper — Joe Pedott — was a homeless Chicago teenager who slept on a cot until SGA intervened. He later gave over $1 million back to SGA and set up a $10M+ charitable foundation to continue giving. It's a genuinely cinematic alumni story: 'Ch-ch-ch-chia!' as a nonprofit fundraising origin story.
candidates.as_of_date_if_known: 2017 grant announcement; alumni relationship confirmed through 2023 obituary coverage
candidates.recency_note: Pedott died June 2023 at 91; story confirmed in multiple 2023 obituaries. The giving relationship spanned decades.

candidates.item: Youth earn $16.50 per hour and receive career coaching and academic support.
candidates.why_interesting: SGA's Lake County Youth Employment Program actually cuts checks — $16.50/hr — to young people (ages 16–24, out of school, no diploma required) whose wages are fully subsidized. It's not a volunteer or stipend program; it's a real paycheck funded through SGA, with career coaching bundled in. Concrete and specific in a field full of vague 'workforce readiness' claims.
candidates.as_of_date_if_known: Listed on 211 Lake County directory (current as of 2025–2026 listing)
candidates.recency_note: Wage figure may be subject to program-year updates; verify before use in outreach.

candidates.why_interesting: SGA runs a program in Roseland — one of Chicago's highest-violence neighborhoods — called 'Cycle Breakers,' pairing mentoring with restorative justice for teens ages 14–18 under their SCAN (Service Coordination and Navigation) umbrella. The program name 'Cycle Breakers' is a sharp, specific counter-branding to their macro framework ('Cycle of Opportunity') and signals a deliberate juvenile-justice intervention, not generic mentoring.
candidates.as_of_date_if_known: Active listing on myChiMyFuture.org as of verification date
candidates.recency_note: Active program listing; confirm program is still enrolling before citing.

candidates.item: In fiscal year 2021, SGA served more than 21,000 children, youth and families.
candidates.why_interesting: 21,000+ people served annually is a meaningful scale figure for a single-city nonprofit — and all services are free to participants. Gives a concrete sense of operational footprint beyond the mission statement.
candidates.recency_note: FY2021 figure — may be out of date; more recent impact numbers may be available on their site or 990.
